title: Add minimum entry confidence gate — block signals below confidence floor
status: QUEUED — blocked until SESSION_EXPOSURE_CAP_001 closes (core/risk.py conflict)
prerequisite_task: SESSION_EXPOSURE_CAP_001 must be APPROVED before pickup (core/risk.py conflict). .env must also be free (BANKROLL_AWARE_SIZING_001 must be APPROVED).
objective: Add a configurable minimum confidence threshold gate. Any signal with confidence below MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE is rejected before the full entry gate waterfall runs. This filters marginal low-edge signals that are effectively coin flips. Default value is 0.60 — below this the model has no meaningful edge on binary outcomes.
context: check_entry_gates() in core/risk.py enforces all entry conditions. Confidence is already used for sizing tiers but is not currently used as a hard block gate. This task adds it as the first gate check — cheapest to evaluate, no DB required. If the signal confidence is below floor, reject immediately with a clear reason string. Confidence lives on the Signal object passed into check_entry_gates().
do_not_touch: Any file not listed in allowed_files above

instructions: This is the minimum confidence required for any signal to be allowed through the entry gate.
Setting to 0.0 effectively disables the gate. This preserves the env-gated disable pattern.
If prior tasks have already edited .env, add this alongside existing entries — do not overwrite them.
Read the full file before editing. Understand the check_entry_gates() function signature and what the signal object looks like.
Add a new env read near the top of the file alongside existing env reads: MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE = float(os.getenv('MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE', '0.60'))
Inside check_entry_gates(), add the confidence floor as the FIRST gate check — before spread, depth, count, and any DB queries. It is the cheapest check and should short-circuit everything else.

Gate logic: signal_confidence = getattr(signal, 'confidence', None). If signal_confidence is None or signal_confidence < MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE: return False, [f'confidence_too_low:{signal_confidence:.3f}<{MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE:.3f}']
If MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE == 0.0, skip the check entirely (gate disabled).
Read the existing gate rejection reason format carefully and match it exactly.
Do not change any other gate logic or thresholds.
Do not change the function signature of check_entry_gates().

acceptance_criteria: core/risk.py reads MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE from env with default 0.60
check_entry_gates() rejects signals with confidence < MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE as its FIRST check
Rejection reason string matches existing gate format
Signal with confidence=None is also rejected (treated as below floor)
All other existing gates are untouched
verification: Read the updated check_entry_gates() and confirm confidence gate is first in the waterfall
Grep risk.py for MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE — expect one env read and one usage
rollback: Remove MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE gate block from check_entry_gates() in risk.py. Remove MIN_ENTRY_CONFIDENCE from .env. Restart bot.
restart_note: Restart required after approval for gate to take effect.
deliverables: Exact lines added to risk.py showing gate position relative to existing gates
